metadata.llm_reasoning: Frontend depends on all listed services; a fault in any of these dependencies could propagate to the symptom frontend, making each a plausible root-to-symptom path. The blast radius is limited to frontend in this topology, but the path plausibly originates from any of its direct dependencies. Given only the topology, multiple equivalent propagation paths exist and the hypothesis that a downstream dependency caused the symptom is structurally supported.

result.synthesis_reasoning: All sources converge on the idea that the frontend is affected by upstream dependencies, but there is no direct error evidence in the available window. The Log Agent provides weak evidence (no logs), while the Topology Agent confirms plausible propagation paths from multiple dependencies. The TCB-RCA analysis centers on the symptom (frontend) itself, offering little help to identify an upstream root cause. Given the absence of concrete logs and the presence of multiple structurally plausible upstream candidates, the most credible root-cause candidates are upstream dependencies of the frontend (checkoutservice, cartservice, productcatalogservice) with a slight preference for checkoutservice due to its criticality in checkout flows and direct dependency relationship.
